summary: Iter9 Sprint 4 — Agency Fleet + GitHub Webhooks backend contracts. 21/22 new assertions GREEN on the public preview URL. Validated: (1) GET /api/fleet/overview for demo (Agency plan) returns fleet_view_enabled=true, workspaces[] each with kpi+apps+plan, rollup{workspaces,apps_total,apps_broken,apps_live,monthly_eur}, generated_at. Apps include status, primary_url, health (ok/down/None), and latency_ms when a monitoring sample exists. Workspaces are sorted problem-first (apps_broken DESC). (2) Free-plan path — downgraded ALL accessible workspaces to 'free' (snapshot+revert) and confirmed GET /fleet/overview returns fleet_view_enabled=false with reason + upgrade_plan='agency'. (3) POST /fleet/bulk-redeploy returns 402 'upgrade to Agency plan' when ineligible. With eligibility + a 'failed' app having a coolify_app_uuid, returns {queued>=1, deployments:[{app_id, deployment_id}]} AND inserts a db.deployments row with trigger='fleet_bulk' (verified directly in Mongo). (4) POST /api/apps issues a 64-char hex webhook_secret, webhook_url={FRONTEND_URL}/api/webhooks/github/{app_id}, webhook_enabled=true. With Cloudflare unconfigured, primary_url stays null and no cloudflare_dns_record_id is set — graceful degradation confirmed. (5) GET /apps/{id}/webhook returns {url, secret, enabled, github_hook_id, auto_registered:false, branch}. (6) POST /webhook/toggle flips enabled true→false→true. (7) POST /webhook/rotate returns a NEW 64-char hex secret and persists it (verified by re-fetching). (8) POST /webhook/register returns {registered:false, reason:'no GitHub token, or repo not accessible'} when no GH OAuth token is connected. (9) POST /api/webhooks/github/{app_id} — invalid signature → 401, valid HMAC+ping → body {status:'pong'} (BUT status_code=202 not 200, see minor issue below), valid HMAC+push to matching branch → 202 with deployment_id+branch (db.deployments row inserted with trigger='webhook'), push to non-matching branch → status='ignored' (also 202 not 200). (10) Nonexistent app id → 404. (11) Auth: fleet endpoints + /apps/.../webhook* require auth (401/403 without session); /api/webhooks/github/{app_id} accepts a session-less request but rejects bad signature with 401 — signature IS the auth. (12) Regression /api/integrations/health returns {coolify, twilio} with no 'whmcs' key — Sprint 3 cleanup intact. Demo workspace 'Acme Studio' verified back on 'agency' plan after the run.

minor.issue: Route decorator declares status_code=202 as default, so ALL non-error returns (ping → body {status:'pong'}, ignored-branch → {status:'ignored'}, disabled-webhook → {status:'disabled'}) emit HTTP 202 instead of 200 as the spec states. Body shapes are correct; only the HTTP status code is off. GitHub treats 2xx as success either way, so this is cosmetic, but the spec under review_request says 'ping returns 200 pong' and 'non-matching branch returns 200 with status=ignored'. Fix: remove status_code=202 from the decorator and return JSONResponse(status_code=202, ...) only on the queued-deploy path.

action_items: (P3 cosmetic) Drop default status_code=202 from @router.post('/webhooks/github/{app_id}') and emit 200 for ping/ignored/disabled responses to match the spec exactly. Functional behavior is correct.

(observability) services/github_webhooks.register_webhook silently returns None when PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL is not https — log at WARNING level which env was missing so admins can diagnose. Currently the log message is generic.

(robustness) routers/webhooks.py builds `commit_sha[:7]` at line 99 without a None-guard around payload['head_commit']. Already covered by `head_commit = payload.get('head_commit') or {}` so head_commit.get('id') can be None — guard exists, but `commit_sha[:7] if commit_sha else 'unknown'` is correct. No issue, just confirmed.
critical_code_review_comments: routers/fleet.py — fleet_overview uses 'ANY accessible workspace eligible' to flip the global flag (L88). Reasonable, but a Pro single-workspace user who happens to be a member of an Agency client's workspace will see ALL their personal workspaces in the fleet. Intentional per docstring, but worth confirming with product.

routers/fleet.py L132 — sorting by `datetime.fromisoformat(...replace('Z','+00:00')).timestamp()` will raise on malformed last_deploy_at strings. Defaults to '1970-01-01T00:00:00+00:00' on None, but a string like '2026-01-15' (date only) would crash the entire endpoint. Recommend wrap in try/except → 0.

routers/fleet.py::bulk_redeploy filters by `coolify_app_uuid: {$ne: None}` — apps that never reached Coolify (first deploy still queued) silently get skipped. Worth logging skipped count in the response so users understand 'queued=0' on an all-failed workspace.

routers/webhooks.py — uses BackgroundTasks for redeploy dispatch, but the function falls back to `_coolify_deploy` when there's no coolify_app_uuid yet (L107-109). That's correct for first-deploy retries triggered by a push, but consider an upper-bound check so a stuck-queued app doesn't get re-queued on every push.

routers/apps.py::create_app L447 — webhook_secret is generated on EVERY create_app even when the user pastes a GitHub-side hook (no auto-register). That's fine, just confirming the secret never leaks because it's only returned to the workspace owner via /apps/{id}/webhook. Good.
services/github_webhooks.py::generate_secret returns secrets.token_hex(32) → 64 hex chars (256 bits). Matches GitHub's recommendation. Good.

services/github_webhooks.py::public_webhook_url falls back to env FRONTEND_URL when PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL is unset (L40-44). Works for this preview env because /api/* is proxied through the same ingress, but in a true production setup the comment is correct and worth keeping.
routers/webhooks.py L37 — _verify_signature uses hmac.compare_digest, so it's constant-time. Good. No timing-leak path.

Code review (cleanup) — routers/apps.py has _auto_register_webhook as a top-level helper but it's only called in 2 places (create_app + rotate). Fine. Just confirming no double-registration race because rotate's background task overwrites webhook_secret AFTER persisting a new local secret, then _auto_register_webhook re-reads it. Correct ordering.
Code review (robustness) — routers/webhooks.py L83 `commit_sha[:7] if commit_sha else 'unknown'` is the only None-guarded slice; verified safe.

success_rate.backend: 95.5% (21/22 — only failure is the cosmetic status_code=202-vs-200 mismatch for ping/ignored returns; functional behavior is correct)

test_credentials: demo@deployhub.dev / demo1234 — verified working. Cookie-based auth via requests.Session, access_token cookie set on /api/auth/login. Acme Studio (ee9ace3a-...) is on 'agency' plan with fleet_view=true (confirmed in Mongo post-run).
seed_data_creation: TestCreateAppWebhookFields creates one app 'TEST_wh_<hex>' on the demo workspace, exercises webhook GET/toggle/rotate/register/ingress, then TestZCleanup DELETEs it. TestFleetOverviewFree + TestBulkRedeploy::test_bulk_redeploy_402_for_non_eligible snapshot every accessible workspace's plan, downgrade ALL to 'free', verify the gate, then revert to original. TestBulkRedeploy::test_bulk_redeploy_eligible_returns_queue temporarily forces ONE app to status='failed' with a fake coolify_app_uuid if missing, then restores. All seeds are fully reverted at end-of-class. Demo workspace plan='agency' confirmed post-run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter9 Sprint 4 (Fleet + GitHub Webhooks) backend is GREEN with one P3 cosmetic. The new test file /app/backend/tests/test_iter9_fleet_webhooks.py has 22 tests across 8 classes — re-runnable as-is. Demo creds + workspace IDs are baked into the file. The only failure is the spec-vs-implementation HTTP-status drift for ping/ignored returns (always 202, spec says 200). If the main agent tightens the route decorator, just delete the assert r.status_code == 200 in test_ping_event_returns_pong (or use status_code in (200, 202) like the non-matching-branch test). Next sprint touching webhooks: verify _auto_register_webhook still no-ops cleanly when GH OAuth is connected for the first time after app creation — the path is exercised by TestCreateAppWebhookFields::test_manual_register_returns_skipped_when_no_token but only the negative branch. Fleet eligibility is keyed on plan.fleet_view — if Pro plan ever flips that bool the gate will open for Pro users; current tests assume only 'agency' has it true.
